verdict_reasoning: HMGB1 is a nuclear/cytoplasmic non-histone chromatin protein with no TM domain, signal peptide, or lipid anchor. It is released as a soluble DAMP during cell death or via unconventional secretion, and acts on extracellular receptors (RAGE, TLR4/2) on other cells, but does not stably anchor to the cell surface of any cell type. Released HMGB1 is soluble and transient, not a wash-resistant surface-bound protein. No CAR-T, ADC, or cell-surface-directed therapeutic program targets HMGB1 anchored to the PM. Cell-state induction, tissue restriction, lysosomal TM anchoring, and stable surface complex partnership are all absent. Secreted-only.
